title: fix(ext/node): fix use-after-free in StatementSync JS iterator
category: GC lifetime use-after-free (iterator outlives its StatementSync wrapper; native statement finalized mid-iteration)
crash_site: SIGSEGV / memory corruption during a `for...of` over the iterator returned by node:sqlite StatementSync.iterate(): a step (next/return) accesses the native statement handle after cppgc finalized the StatementSync wrapper. The native next/return code is correct; it was handed a finalized statement.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the iterator's next_func / return_func (they crash but are correct — they just deref the handle); the cppgc finalizer for StatementSync. The bug is iterate() building an iterator that does NOT keep the wrapper alive.
ground_truth.fix_summary: StatementSync::iterate() returns a JS iterator object that references the native next_func/return_func but holds NO reference to the StatementSync wrapper (args.this()). cppgc can therefore collect the wrapper — finalizing the native statement handle — while the iterator is still live, leaving next/return with a dangling pointer (use-after-free). Fix: store a hidden `__STATEMENT_REF` property on the iterator pointing at the wrapper, so the reference chain iterator -> statement keeps the handle alive for the iterator's lifetime.

admission.note: monogram+haiku vs baseline+haiku discrimination. HARD: crash is in the iterator step touching a finalized statement, but the cause is iterate() omitting a strong reference from the iterator to its StatementSync wrapper, letting GC finalize the handle mid-iteration. Live decoy = the crashing next/return code. Authored from PR #31770 fix diff (add __STATEMENT_REF = args.this()).

provenance: https://github.com/denoland/deno/pull/31770 — fix(ext/node) UAF in StatementSync JS iterator (merged 2026-01-08); fix commit 8d47d7e24402; base 7558cafe636c. Root cause + fix from the commit diff (StatementSync::iterate adds __STATEMENT_REF wrapper ref).
